title: AI Productivity Workflows for Study and Work

blocks.title: Week 8 Focus
blocks.content: Students redesign manual tasks into guided AI-supported workflows.

items.label: Repeatability
items.description: Design a process that can be used more than once.

items.label: Review
items.description: Add a human checkpoint before final use.

items.label: Input
items.description: The notes, text, or request that starts the process.

items.label: AI Step
items.description: The model action such as summarize or draft.

items.label: Review
items.description: The human check before using the output.

items.label: Output
items.description: The final document, list, or recommendation.

blocks.title: Design Rule
blocks.content: A simple workflow with a clear review step is better than a flashy workflow no one can trust.

activity.title: Workflow Redesign Studio
activity.instructions: Break a common task into input, AI step, review step, and output.
activity.success_criteria: Produce a complete workflow with a clear review point.

questions.prompt: What is a workflow in this course context?
options: A repeatable sequence of steps for completing a task
questions.explanation: A workflow is a repeatable process with clear steps and outputs.

questions.prompt: Which element should be included in an AI-assisted workflow?
options: A clear input, AI step, and output
questions.explanation: Useful workflows define inputs, processing steps, outputs, and usually a review step.

questions.prompt: Why add human review to a workflow?
options: To slow it down with no benefit
To check quality, accuracy, and safety
To remove the need for any tools
questions.explanation: Human review helps catch mistakes and keeps the workflow accountable.

reflection.prompt: Describe one study or work task you could turn into an AI-assisted workflow, and name the review point.
reflection.placeholder: I could turn meeting notes into action items, then a human reviews the final list before sharing it.

teacher_notes: Use realistic academic or office tasks instead of abstract examples.
Ask students where the workflow can fail and where review is required.
